content.text: I'll help you list all your Mittwald projects and their MySQL databases. Let me start by fetching your projects.

input.description: List all Mittwald projects and MySQL databases
input.prompt:
You have access to Mittwald MCP tools. Your task is to:

1. First, call mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_list to get all projects
2. For each project returned, extract the project ID
3. For each project ID, call mcp__mittwald__mittwald_database_list_my_sql_databases with that projectId to get all MySQL databases
4. Compile the complete results showing:
   - Project name and ID for each project
   - For each project, list all its MySQL databases with: database name, database ID, version, and status

Return the complete structured data showing all projects and their databases in a clear format.

content.text:
You have access to Mittwald MCP tools. Your task is to:

1. First, call mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_list to get all projects
2. For each project returned, extract the project ID
3. For each project ID, call mcp__mittwald__mittwald_database_list_my_sql_databases with that projectId to get all MySQL databases
4. Compile the complete results showing:
   - Project name and ID for each project
   - For each project, list all its MySQL databases with: database name, database ID, version, and status

Return the complete structured data showing all projects and their databases in a clear format.

input.description: Get list of all Mittwald projects

tool_use_result:
Error: Exit code 1
error: unknown command 'call'

content.content:
Usage: claude mcp [options] [command]

Configure and manage MCP servers

Options:
  -h, --help                                     Display help for command

Commands:
  serve [options]                                Start the Claude Code MCP server
  add [options] <name> <commandOrUrl> [args...]  Add an MCP server to Claude Code.
  
  Examples:
    # Add HTTP server:
    claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp
  
    # Add SSE server:
    claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse
  
    # Add stdio server:
    claude mcp add --transport stdio airtable --env AIRTABLE_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server
  remove [options] <name>                        Remove an MCP server
  list                                           List configured MCP servers
  get <name>                                     Get details about an MCP server
  add-json [options] <name> <json>               Add an MCP server (stdio or SSE) with a JSON string
  add-from-claude-desktop [options]              Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop (Mac and WSL only)
  reset-project-choices                          Reset all approved and rejected project-scoped (.mcp.json) servers within this project
  help [command]                                 display help for command

tool_use_result.stdout:
Usage: claude mcp [options] [command]

Configure and manage MCP servers

Options:
  -h, --help                                     Display help for command

Commands:
  serve [options]                                Start the Claude Code MCP server
  add [options] <name> <commandOrUrl> [args...]  Add an MCP server to Claude Code.
  
  Examples:
    # Add HTTP server:
    claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp
  
    # Add SSE server:
    claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse
  
    # Add stdio server:
    claude mcp add --transport stdio airtable --env AIRTABLE_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server
  remove [options] <name>                        Remove an MCP server
  list                                           List configured MCP servers
  get <name>                                     Get details about an MCP server
  add-json [options] <name> <json>               Add an MCP server (stdio or SSE) with a JSON string
  add-from-claude-desktop [options]              Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop (Mac and WSL only)
  reset-project-choices                          Reset all approved and rejected project-scoped (.mcp.json) servers within this project
  help [command]                                 display help for command

input.command:
cat > /tmp/mcp_request.json << 'EOF'
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": 1,
  "method": "tools/list"
}
EOF
curl -s -X POST https://mittwald-mcp-fly2.fly.dev/mcp \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @/tmp/mcp_request.json | jq -r '.result.tools[] | select(.name | contains("project")) | .name' | head -10
input.description: List available MCP tools via direct API

tool_use_result.prompt:
You have access to Mittwald MCP tools. Your task is to:

1. First, call mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_list to get all projects
2. For each project returned, extract the project ID
3. For each project ID, call mcp__mittwald__mittwald_database_list_my_sql_databases with that projectId to get all MySQL databases
4. Compile the complete results showing:
   - Project name and ID for each project
   - For each project, list all its MySQL databases with: database name, database ID, version, and status

Return the complete structured data showing all projects and their databases in a clear format.
